change: Add a parameter to core.IntrinsicGas(...); every call site must pass the new argument.

impact.why: Calls IntrinsicGas(msg.Data, msg.AccessList, msg.SetCodeAuthorizations, contractCreation, rules.IsHomestead, rules.IsIstanbul, rules.IsShanghai) at l.478 to subtract intrinsic gas; primary consensus-path caller, must supply new arg.
core.IntrinsicGas at l.111 to reject txs below the minimum; mempool admission must pass the new parameter.
core.IntrinsicGas at l.136 computing per-tx intrinsic gas for the t9n tool report; must pass new arg.
core.IntrinsicGas at l.58 in the consensus transaction-test harness; encodes a real intrinsic-gas contract (not a unit _test.go), so included.

grep_would_miss: Nothing material — IntrinsicGas is a free function always called by its exported name, so a name-grep finds every call site. Indirect ErrIntrinsicGas references (gasestimator.go:197, validation.go:116, error.go, several _test.go) are NOT call sites of IntrinsicGas and are correctly NOT in the impact set.
notes: Adding a parameter is a pure signature/compile break, so impact == exact call sites. Three non-test callers (state_transition, txpool/validation, t8ntool/transaction) plus tests/transaction_test_util.go which is a .go (not _test.go) consensus-test helper encoding the intrinsic-gas rule. Pure _test.go callers (core/bench_test.go:86, state_processor_test.go) were excluded. cks find_callers(core.IntrinsicGas) overflowed the token cap (result saved to disk) but ripgrep gives a complete and verified call-site list, which I confirmed by Read.
